exercises.title: All Seven Operators
exercises.description: Practice every arithmetic operator. Print the result of each operation on a and b: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, floor division, modulo, and exponentiation.
exercises.starterCode:
a = 17
b = 5

# TODO: Print a + b
# TODO: Print a - b
# TODO: Print a * b
# TODO: Print a / b
# TODO: Print a // b   (floor division)
# TODO: Print a % b    (modulo / remainder)
# TODO: Print a ** b   (exponentiation)
hints: Floor division (//) divides and drops the decimal part.
Modulo (%) gives the remainder after division: 17 // 5 = 3 remainder 2, so 17 % 5 = 2.

exercises.title: Operator Precedence
exercises.description: Python follows order of operations (PEMDAS). Print the result of each expression. Think before running — predict first!
exercises.starterCode:
# TODO: Print 2 + 3 * 4
# TODO: Print (2 + 3) * 4
# TODO: Print 10 - 2 + 3
# TODO: Print 2 ** 3 + 1
# TODO: Print 20 / 4 - 1
hints: Multiplication (*) runs before addition (+) without parentheses.
Parentheses override the default order — they go first.

exercises.title: Practical Calculations
exercises.description: Use math operators to solve real problems. Calculate and print the results using the given variables.
exercises.starterCode:
# Cookie distribution
cookies = 23
people = 4

# TODO: Print how many cookies each person gets (floor division)
# TODO: Print how many cookies are left over (modulo)

# Temperature conversion
celsius = 100.0
# Formula: fahrenheit = (celsius * 9/5) + 32
# TODO: Calculate fahrenheit and print it
hints: Each person gets cookies // people whole cookies.
For the temperature formula, use parentheses to group: (celsius * 9/5) + 32

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Floor Division and Modulo
warmup.description: These two operators work together like division with a remainder. What does this print?
warmup.starterCode:
# Predict before running
print(17 // 5)
print(17 % 5)
print(2 ** 8)
